title: MAMATA BANERJEE VS BJP: SIR CONTROVERSY EXPLAINED
description:
Did BJP win the West Bengal election because of SIR? Mamata Banerjee and TMC have alleged that genuine voters were deleted from the voter list before the election under the Special Intensive Revision process. Reports say around 91 lakh names were removed from Bengal’s voter list, and TMC claimed in the Supreme Court that deletions were higher than victory margins in 31 seats.

But Election Commission data shows a more complicated picture. In several high-deletion seats like Sujapur, Raghunathganj, Samserganj, Ratua and Suti, TMC itself performed strongly. The Supreme Court also asked TMC to file a fresh plea if it has proof that the election result was impacted.

In this video, we explain what SIR is, why voter names are deleted, what TMC is alleging, what the data shows, and whether BJP’s Bengal win can really be linked only to SIR.
